trap: Behavioral high/low-side driver subckt with a model-specific, roughly VCC-independent ~5 V logic threshold. A 3.3 V logic drive never crosses it, so the output stays idle while the input toggles -- and a single-device find_spice_model --verify (load + op-point) passes anyway because it never checks the threshold.
detect: Op-point the driver in an ISOLATED harness and probe IN vs the datasheet logic threshold; sweep input amplitude to find where HO/LO start switching.
workaround: Level-shift the logic input up to the threshold (a 2N7000 common-source inverter turns a 3.3 V swing into 0/12 V); raise VCC above UVLO; assert the enable/SD pin.

trap: Stiff vendor MOSFET subckt (POWMOS.LIB). Two distinct failures: (1) inside a high-gain (Aol~1e6) DC control loop the Newton solver has no basin at mid-range setpoints even though the extremes converge ('No convergence in dc analysis'); (2) under hard commutation its internal capacitances stall the integrator ('Timestep too small; trouble with xq1:dmos-instance').
detect: Op-point / .op converges at setpoint extremes but not mid-range; transient aborts on the switching edge with a dmos-instance timestep-too-small tail.
workaround: UIC transient settle (seed loop nodes at 0, charge a ~10 nF cap ~20 ms, tail-average) instead of .op at the failing setpoints; RC snubber (~100 Ohm + 680 pF) + gate series R to tame dv/dt in switching runs. NEVER add a large conditioning resistor across the pass device -- it corrupts Vout.

trap: CMOS-input op-amp macromodel whose internal MOSFETs will not converge in a multi-instance transient feedback loop even with stiff=True + UIC -- the transient timestep collapses ('trouble with mosfet-instance m.x in op-amp macromodel'). A single-device op-point verify does not predict this; the collapse only appears once several instances close a loop.
detect: --verify LOADS and the op-point converges, but the full transient collapses once >1 instance is wired into a feedback loop.
workaround: Swap the oscillator/loop core to a bipolar-input model (LT1364-class); reduce the instance count; use bipolar supply rails so the model biases in-range.

trap: None known in these designs -- this is the bipolar-input part the CMOS-macromodel convergence failures were resolved by swapping TO. Converges in multi-instance transient feedback loops where CMOS-input macromodels (LMC6482-class) collapse.
workaround: Prefer for oscillator/loop cores and telemetry buffers; note it is high-GBW (~70 MHz) so still roll it off on unbypassed dividers next to switching nodes (see opamp-buffer-rails-switching).
